series_metadata.series_description: If an individual is involved in a motor vehicle crash and the property damages were more than $400 or bodily injury was sustained, they could have a two-year suspension of their driving and registration privileges. In order to have these privileges reinstated they must meet one of the following requirements in addition to satisfying all reinstatement fees and proof of financial responsibility insurance for a period of three to five years. 1. Submit a State of Ohio Motor Vehicle Crash Report (BMV 3303) signed by their insurance agent which shows they had insurance at the time of the collision, or 2. Submit a release or agreement to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles signed by both parties, or 3. Make a security deposit with the BMV in the full amount of damages. All incoming correspondence relating to the above suspension are stored in the image case folder.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Paper: All paper documents are scanned into image and held 30 days and ; Electronic: IT should scan files, if case has been closed , delete records. All cases generated on/or January, 1989 are stored in imaging. from close date
